test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill can execute a complete, well-defined product-based side hustle from idea to recommendation. This is the primary happy path and tests all 7 phases in sequence.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: End-to-end execution: idea validation, market validation, cost identification, break-even analysis, scenario projections, sensitivity analysis, and recommendation generation. Tests that all outputs are populated with realistic data.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If any phase fails to execute, outputs are missing, or calculations are incorrect (e.g., cumulative cash doesn't match break-even month), the test will fail. This catches fundamental skill failures.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords span all major outputs (idea_summary, market_validation, startup_costs_breakdown, etc.) and ensure the skill produces the complete financial model as specified.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill handles service-based businesses (different cost structure than products). Tests that the skill correctly identifies strong unit economics and quick break-even for low-startup-cost businesses.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Service-specific logic: minimal startup costs, low variable costs, high contribution margin, quick profitability. Tests that the skill recommends 'Go' for viable business models.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill incorrectly calculates unit economics or recommends 'No-Go' for a viable service business, this test fails. Catches cost structure mishandling.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords focus on unit economics (unit_contribution_margin, profitability, feasible) and recommendation (Go), validating that the skill correctly identifies strong business models.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 1 quality gate for idea specificity. Validates that the skill rejects vague inputs and requests clarification rather than proceeding with a bad model.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error handling in Phase 1: the skill must detect when idea_description, target_market, or revenue_model lack specificity and return an 'Adjust' response with guidance.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill proceeds with vague inputs or returns a generic error, this test fails. Catches failure to enforce quality gates.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'Adjust', 'specificity', 'rejection', and 'clarification_needed', validating that the skill correctly rejects bad inputs and guides the user.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 4 error handler for negative unit economics. This is a critical failure mode: if variable cost > price, the business is not viable and the skill must abort.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error handling in Phase 4: the skill must detect when variable_cost_per_unit >= price_per_unit and return an 'Abort' response with guidance to increase price or reduce COGS.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill proceeds with negative margin or returns a generic error, this test fails. Catches failure to validate unit economics.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'Abort', 'not_viable', 'negative_margin', 'do_not_proceed', validating that the skill correctly aborts and prevents the user from building a model for a broken business.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 4 error handler for unrealistic unit volume estimates. New side hustles should start with conservative estimates (5-15 units/month), not 500 units/month.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error handling in Phase 4: the skill must detect when estimated monthly units are unrealistic for a brand-new side hustle and cap at 50 units/month, then ask the user to justify higher estimates.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill accepts 500 units/month for a brand-new business without validation, the resulting model will be misleading. This test catches unrealistic projections.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'Adjust', 'unrealistic', 'capped', 'conservative_assumption', 'market_validation_needed', validating that the skill enforces realistic assumptions.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the break-even analysis quality gate at a boundary condition (break-even at month 12). Validates that the skill correctly identifies profitability month and flags long-term investments.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Break-even analysis: the skill must correctly calculate when cumulative cash turns positive, identify month 12 as the profitability month, and recommend 'Conditional' for long-term investments.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill miscalculates break-even month or recommends 'Go' for a 12-month break-even without funding, this test fails. Catches break-even calculation errors.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'break_even_analysis', 'month_12', 'profitability_achieved', 'long_term_investment', 'Conditional', validating correct break-even identification and recommendation.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 2 error handler for missing market validation. Validates that the skill flags risk and recommends validation methods when no evidence of customer demand exists.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error handling in Phase 2: the skill must detect when no market validation has been conducted, assign confidence_level: 'low', set risk_flag: true, and recommend a validation method.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill proceeds without flagging risk or recommends 'Go' for an unvalidated idea, this test fails. Catches failure to enforce market validation importance.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'confidence_level_low', 'risk_flag_true', 'validation_method', 'Conditional', 'validate_demand_before_launch', validating that the skill correctly flags unvalidated ideas.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 7 quality gate for time commitment validation. Validates that the skill detects when projected hours exceed available hours and flags as infeasible.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Time commitment validation in Phase 7: the skill must calculate hours_per_unit, project hours needed in month 6, compare to available_hours_per_week, and flag if infeasible.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill doesn't validate time commitment or recommends 'Go' for an infeasible business, this test fails. Catches failure to validate execution feasibility.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'time_commitment_validation', 'feasible_false', 'flag_true', 'exceeds_available', validating that the skill correctly identifies time commitment issues.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 7 sensitivity analysis quality gate. Validates that the skill identifies when profitability is highly sensitive to a single variable and flags as critical success factor.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Sensitivity analysis in Phase 7: the skill must calculate impact of ±10% price change, ±20% volume change, ±15% cost change, identify the top 3 drivers, and flag if one driver dominates.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill doesn't perform sensitivity analysis or misses a critical driver, this test fails. Catches failure to identify business fragility.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'sensitivity_analysis', 'price_sensitivity', 'high_impact', 'fragile', 'critical_success_factor', validating that the skill correctly identifies key drivers.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill handles digital products (different cost structure than physical products) and correctly incorporates existing market validation into the recommendation.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Digital product-specific logic: minimal startup costs, minimal variable costs, high contribution margin, immediate profitability. Tests that the skill recommends 'Go' when market validation is strong.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill incorrectly calculates digital product economics or ignores market validation, this test fails. Catches digital product mishandling.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'confidence_level_high', 'risk_flag_false', 'break_even_month_1', 'Go', 'strong_unit_economics', validating that the skill correctly handles validated digital products.

test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error handling in Phase 1: the skill must detect when idea_description, target_market, or revenue_model lack specificity and return an 'Adjust' response with guidance.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill proceeds with vague inputs or returns a generic error, this test fails. Catches failure to enforce quality gates.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'Adjust', 'specificity', 'rejection', and 'clarification_needed', validating that the skill correctly rejects bad inputs and guides the user.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 4 error handler for negative unit economics. This is a critical failure mode: if variable cost > price, the business is not viable and the skill must abort.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error handling in Phase 4: the skill must detect when variable_cost_per_unit >= price_per_unit and return an 'Abort' response with guidance to increase price or reduce COGS.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill proceeds with negative margin or returns a generic error, this test fails. Catches failure to validate unit economics.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'Abort', 'not_viable', 'negative_margin', 'do_not_proceed', validating that the skill correctly aborts and prevents the user from building a model for a broken business.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 4 error handler for unrealistic unit volume estimates. New side hustles should start with conservative estimates (5-15 units/month), not 500 units/month.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error handling in Phase 4: the skill must detect when estimated monthly units are unrealistic for a brand-new side hustle and cap at 50 units/month, then ask the user to justify higher estimates.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill accepts 500 units/month for a brand-new business without validation, the resulting model will be misleading. This test catches unrealistic projections.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'Adjust', 'unrealistic', 'capped', 'conservative_assumption', 'market_validation_needed', validating that the skill enforces realistic assumptions.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the break-even analysis quality gate at a boundary condition (break-even at month 12). Validates that the skill correctly identifies profitability month and flags long-term investments.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Break-even analysis: the skill must correctly calculate when cumulative cash turns positive, identify month 12 as the profitability month, and recommend 'Conditional' for long-term investments.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill miscalculates break-even month or recommends 'Go' for a 12-month break-even without funding, this test fails. Catches break-even calculation errors.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'break_even_analysis', 'month_12', 'profitability_achieved', 'long_term_investment', 'Conditional', validating correct break-even identification and recommendation.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 2 error handler for missing market validation. Validates that the skill flags risk and recommends validation methods when no evidence of customer demand exists.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error handling in Phase 2: the skill must detect when no market validation has been conducted, assign confidence_level: 'low', set risk_flag: true, and recommend a validation method.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill proceeds without flagging risk or recommends 'Go' for an unvalidated idea, this test fails. Catches failure to enforce market validation importance.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'confidence_level_low', 'risk_flag_true', 'validation_method', 'Conditional', 'validate_demand_before_launch', validating that the skill correctly flags unvalidated ideas.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 7 quality gate for time commitment validation. Validates that the skill detects when projected hours exceed available hours and flags as infeasible.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Time commitment validation in Phase 7: the skill must calculate hours_per_unit, project hours needed in month 6, compare to available_hours_per_week, and flag if infeasible.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill doesn't validate time commitment or recommends 'Go' for an infeasible business, this test fails. Catches failure to validate execution feasibility.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'time_commitment_validation', 'feasible_false', 'flag_true', 'exceeds_available', validating that the skill correctly identifies time commitment issues.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 7 sensitivity analysis quality gate. Validates that the skill identifies when profitability is highly sensitive to a single variable and flags as critical success factor.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Sensitivity analysis in Phase 7: the skill must calculate impact of ±10% price change, ±20% volume change, ±15% cost change, identify the top 3 drivers, and flag if one driver dominates.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill doesn't perform sensitivity analysis or misses a critical driver, this test fails. Catches failure to identify business fragility.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'sensitivity_analysis', 'price_sensitivity', 'high_impact', 'fragile', 'critical_success_factor', validating that the skill correctly identifies key drivers.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill handles digital products (different cost structure than physical products) and correctly incorporates existing market validation into the recommendation.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Digital product-specific logic: minimal startup costs, minimal variable costs, high contribution margin, immediate profitability. Tests that the skill recommends 'Go' when market validation is strong.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If the skill incorrectly calculates digital product economics or ignores market validation, this test fails. Catches digital product mishandling.
test_rationale.keywords_justify: Keywords include 'confidence_level_high', 'risk_flag_false', 'break_even_month_1', 'Go', 'strong_unit_economics', validating that the skill correctly handles validated digital products.

chain_definition.name: side-hustle-financial-modeler-chain
chain_definition.description: Transform a side hustle idea into a comprehensive financial model with market validation, startup costs, break-even analysis, 12-month projections, and three-scenario planning.

entry_inputs.name: idea_description
entry_inputs.description: Plain-English description of the side hustle concept

entry_inputs.name: available_hours_per_week
entry_inputs.description: Hours per week available for side hustle (default: 10)

entry_inputs.name: growth_assumption_base
entry_inputs.description: Monthly growth rate for base case, 0.0-0.30 (default: 0.10)

goal_requirements.requirement: Validate that customer demand exists for the side hustle idea
Identify and quantify all startup costs
Identify and quantify all monthly operating costs
Calculate break-even point (units, revenue, timeline)
Validate time commitment feasibility
Project 12-month financials for base case scenario
Project 12-month financials for best case scenario
Project 12-month financials for worst case scenario
Compare scenarios side-by-side
Identify key financial drivers and their sensitivity
Document all assumptions with confidence levels
Identify critical success factors
Provide Go/No-Go/Conditional recommendation

execution_plan.title: Side Hustle Financial Modeler - Execution Plan
execution_plan.description: Single-step pipeline executing the comprehensive side-hustle financial modeling skill

execution_layers.description: Execute side-hustle-financial-modeler skill with all 7 phases sequentially

critical_path.description: Single skill execution path determines overall chain duration

execution_plan.parallelism_diagram:
LAYER 1:
  [step_1: side-hustle-financial-modeler]
    ├─ Phase 1: Idea Validation & Scope Definition
    ├─ Phase 2: Market Validation
    ├─ Phase 3: Startup Costs Identification
    ├─ Phase 4: Monthly Operating Costs & Revenue Model Refinement
    ├─ Phase 5: Break-Even Analysis & Funding Requirement
    ├─ Phase 6: Scenario Projections (Base, Best, Worst)
    └─ Phase 7: Scenario Synthesis, Sensitivity Analysis & Recommendation

No parallel execution possible (single skill, sequential phases)
execution_plan.data_flow_summary: Linear flow: User inputs → Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3 → Phase 4 → Phase 5 → Phase 6 → Phase 7 → 14 outputs
notes: This chain is a single-skill pipeline; all work is done within the side-hustle-financial-modeler skill
The skill is self-contained and requires no external data sources or chaining with other skills
User must provide three required inputs: idea_description, target_market, revenue_model
Four optional inputs have sensible defaults (available_hours_per_week=10, projection_months=12, growth_assumption_base=0.10)
All 14 outputs are produced by the single skill execution
Execution is sequential (7 phases must run in order); no parallelization possible

audit_notes.rationale: The workflow_description maps perfectly to a single, well-designed skill (side-hustle-financial-modeler). No additional skills are needed. The skill definition is comprehensive, with 7 sequential phases covering all requirements (market validation, cost analysis, break-even, projections, sensitivity analysis, and recommendation). The improved skill definition addresses all audit issues from the original version.
audit_notes.skill_coverage: 100% of workflow requirements are covered by the single side-hustle-financial-modeler skill
design_decisions: Single-skill chain: The workflow is best executed as a monolithic skill rather than a multi-skill pipeline, because all phases are tightly coupled (each phase depends on outputs from prior phases) and the skill is designed as a cohesive procedure
No external data sources: The skill is self-contained; it does not require data from external APIs, databases, or other skills
No chaining needed: The workflow does not benefit from decomposition into smaller skills; the side-hustle-financial-modeler skill is the appropriate level of abstraction
improvements_from_audit: Market validation phase added (Phase 2) to confirm customer demand before financial modeling
Time commitment validation added (Phase 7) to cross-check projected unit volume against available hours
Funding requirement calculation added (Phase 5) to quantify capital needs if break-even > 6 months
Assumption log added (Phase 7) to document all assumptions with confidence levels
Sensitivity analysis specificity improved: price ±10%, volume ±20%, costs ±15%
Quality gates made measurable and objective (removed subjective language like 'specific enough')
Error handling expanded to cover all failure modes with clear response strategies
Reference section added with business type benchmarks, break-even interpretation, scenario growth assumptions, market validation methods, critical success factors template, and Go/No-Go decision tree
